task_completion.description: Create a calculated table named 'Customer_Profiles' containing Customer ID, Customer Name, and Last_Purchase_Date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Power BI Data or Model view, or the Fields pane, a table named 'Customer_Profiles' is visible with the specified columns.

task_completion.description: Add 'Recency_Days' and 'Status' calculated columns to the 'Customer_Profiles' table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Customer_Profiles' table in the Fields pane shows 'Recency_Days' and 'Status' columns, typically indicated by the calculated column icon.
The page tab at the bottom of the Power BI report view is named 'Retention Strategy'.

task_completion.description: Create a donut chart showing the count of customers by Status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A donut chart visual is present on the canvas, with its legend or tooltips indicating it displays customer counts categorized by 'Status'.

task_completion.description: Create a table visual displaying Customer Name, Recency_Days, and Status, filtered to show only '3. Lost' customers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A table visual is visible on the canvas with the specified columns. The contents of the table or the Filters pane should show it is filtered to only display '3. Lost' customers.

task_completion.description: Save the Power BI report as 'Customer_Retention.pbix' and export the filtered list to 'lost_customers.csv'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI window title shows 'Customer_Retention.pbix' and a file explorer, desktop view, or export dialog shows 'lost_customers.csv' saved in the Desktop folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the Power BI GUI to create the report and visuals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Power BI Desktop application open with active interactions (e.g., clicking menus, dragging fields, writing DAX), rather than just terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The visuals are genuinely rendered by Power BI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The donut chart and table look like standard Power BI visuals, not mockups or images pasted into a different application.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create empty files with the target names using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation of the pbix and csv files should be the result of saving and exporting from within Power BI, not via PowerShell or bash commands like 'New-Item' or 'touch'.
